title: The Cup of Cold Water
subtitle: Christ promises that even a cup of cold water, given in His name, shall not lose its reward

stillness: Be still, and come away from the noise of the world.
Quiet your heart before the Lord who sees the smallest gift.
He weighs not the hand, but the will.
gospel: He who receives you, receives me;
and he who receives me, receives him who sent me.
He who receives a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet's reward;
and he who receives a just man in the name of a just man shall receive a just man's reward.
And whosoever shall give to drink to one of these little ones a cup of cold water only, in the name of a disciple,
amen I say to you, he shall not lose his reward.
behold: Behold how the Lord, who separates his disciples from friends and from earthly goods,
gives them others in their stead for consolation:
those who receive them in love, who open their houses to them,
and supply what is needful for life, for the hope of an eternal reward.
For he says: he who receives you bodily, receives me spiritually;
you are my members, and I am in you.
A certain preacher cried out, not a little, in his preaching,
that the love and devotion of many had grown cold toward God and his Saints,
and that Christ now no longer found his Martha in the world,
who once rendered him so many services as of old.
When the sermon was ended, a devout matron came to him,
and, as if rebuking him, said:
O brother, if Martha now found her Christ as of old,
truly Christ now also, as of old, would find his Martha.
consider: Consider what a great reward this is:
that by receiving a man, you are made the dwelling-place of the whole Trinity.
For he who venerates and receives God's messenger receives the Father, the Son, and the entire Trinity;
but he who does injury to God's minister does injury to Almighty God and the inseparable Trinity.
Let no man excuse himself by his poverty.
By a most light commandment the Lord has wiped away that excuse, saying:
whosoever shall give a cup — not a flagon;
of water — not of wine;
of cold only — not hot;
to one — not to many;
of these my least ones — he shall not lose his reward.
He says cold water, that none expend even the labour of warming it,
lest the poor man complain he had no wood to make it hot.
Whoever, then, however poor he be, can do some work of mercy,
for he can give a drink of cold water.
God regards not how much you give, but out of how much you give it.
A truly just Judge!
For he who set a penalty even for an idle word,
it was just that he should give a reward even for cold water.
If you wished to give more and could not — is it just
that the generous will be cramped to fit the meagre work?
Better that the work be stretched to fit the will,
than that the will be narrowed to fit the work.
Even to bare willing, without the work, I think he will render the reward.
prayer: grant me so to venerate and receive, for your name's sake,
the prophets, who preach you by their teaching,
and the just, who show you forth by a good life,
and your disciples and messengers, who in any way proclaim you;
and to render them the services and good offices of love,
that I may merit, by your gift, to obtain a reward together with them, through their merits.
Grant me also, Lord my God,
reverently to hear prelates, preachers, and priests,
and by obeying to acquiesce in their admonitions,
that with the truly obedient I may be able to find grace before you.

latinJewel.latin: Non quantum des, sed ex quanto, attendit Deus.
latinJewel.english: God regards not how much you give, but out of how much you give it.
